sprintName: Phase Modules: Planner, Contract, Generator, Evaluator
summary: Sprint 4 ports the four agent-phase modules (planner, contract, generator, evaluator) to Python 3.8+ stdlib. All 35 contract criteria pass. The four load-bearing tolerance helpers (parse_decision/is_accepted, count_criteria, _get_result, _get_count) accept all required field-name variants and case-insensitive values, exactly mirroring the bash jq-cascade semantics. Generator's blocked-status return code 2 is preserved (load-bearing for the upcoming orchestrator). All 117 layer1 tests pass via `python tests/run-all.py layer1`. No .sh or .ps1 files were modified. Stdlib-only imports verified by AST scan. Bats coverage parity is satisfied: every bats @test has a Python equivalent (Python adds tolerance-helper unit tests on top).

criteriaResults.evidence: harness/lib/planner.py defines invoke_planner(mode='new') and tests/layer1/test_planner.py::test_returns_sprint_count exercises it with MOCK_CLAUDE_SCENARIO=pass; passes.
test_creates_product_spec_md and test_creates_sprint_plan_json verify both outputs; sprint-plan.json is json.load-able with .sprints list.
test_logs_mock_invocation asserts 'agent=planner' substring in MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG.
test_extend_mode_prompt_contains_extend asserts 'extend' substring in mock log; planner.py:65 selects _EXTEND_PROMPT for mode=='extend'.
planner.py:94-100 reads projectType, log_success when design-spec.md present and log_warn when absent; no exception raised in either branch.
harness/lib/contract.py exposes negotiate_contract(sprint_num); test_creates_contract_json_on_acceptance calls it with int 1; no exception.
contract.py:185 shutil.copyfile(proposal -> contract.json) on acceptance; test_creates_contract_json_on_acceptance asserts file exists.
test_creates_proposal_and_review asserts both contract-proposal.json and contract-review.json exist after a single round.
test_invokes_both_generator_and_evaluator asserts MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG contains both 'agent=generator' and 'agent=evaluator'; contract.py invokes both via invoke_claude.
test_revise_then_accept_takes_two_rounds asserts grep count == 2 of 'agent=generator' under MOCK_CLAUDE_SCENARIO=revise-then-accept and contract.json exists.

contract.py:52-69 parse_decision/is_accepted; verified via tests test_decision_{accepted,accept,approved,approve,review_verdict,verdict,case_insensitive,unknown_is_rejected} and direct execution: all variants in {decision,reviewVerdict,verdict} accept {accepted,accept,approved,approve,ACCEPTED}.

contract.py:72-105 count_criteria handles .criteria list, .features[].acceptanceCriteria, .features[].criteria, flat .acceptanceCriteria, and 0 for empty; verified by tests test_count_from_criteria_list, test_count_from_features_acceptanceCriteria, test_count_from_flat_acceptanceCriteria, test_count_empty_returns_zero plus direct execution including the features+criteria fallback.

contract.py:201-205 max-rounds fallback shutil.copyfile latest proposal to contract.json and returns True (the bash 'return 0' equivalent at the Python boolean layer); negotiate_contract returns True after MAX_CONTRACT_ROUNDS exhaustion.
harness/lib/generator.py:68 def invoke_generator(sprint_num, attempt: int = 1) -> int; signature inspected via inspect.signature confirms attempt default 1.
test_creates_status_json verifies status.json exists with .status=='ready-for-eval' on the pass scenario.
test_creates_generator_log_md verifies sprint-01/generator-log.md exists after invoke_generator.
test_returns_0_on_success asserts invoke_generator(1,1) == 0 with MOCK_CLAUDE_SCENARIO=pass.
generator.py:109-113 returns EXIT_BLOCKED (=2) when status=='blocked'; test_returns_2_when_blocked asserts return == 2 under fail-generator-blocked scenario. CRITICAL invariant preserved.
test_logs_invocation asserts MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG contains 'agent=generator'.
generator.py:38-65 _build_prompt branches on attempt > 1 and eval_report_present to add retry references; invoke_claude carries the prompt to the mock which records it.
generator.py:53-58 appends design-spec.md instruction when design_spec_present is true; controlled by HARNESS_STATE/design-spec.md presence check at line 88.
harness/lib/evaluator.py exposes both invoke_evaluator(sprint_num, attempt=1) and invoke_regression(); both callable per test imports.
test_returns_true_on_pass asserts invoke_evaluator(1,1) returns truthy on MOCK_CLAUDE_SCENARIO=pass (bash 0 semantics).
test_returns_false_on_fail asserts invoke_evaluator returns falsy on fail-eval scenario (bash 1 semantics).
test_creates_eval_report_json verifies eval-report.json exists and json.load yields a dict with overallResult/result/verdict-tolerated key present.
test_logs_invocation asserts MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG contains 'agent=evaluator'.

evaluator.py:45-52 _get_result lowercases value of overallResult|result|verdict; tests test_overallResult_field, test_result_field, test_verdict_field, test_pass_case_insensitive cover {PASS,pass,Passed,passed} across all three field names; FAIL/missing return non-pass.

evaluator.py:55-70 _get_count walks dotted-path keys; tests test_pass_count_direct, test_pass_count_snake, test_pass_count_score_nested, test_count_returns_zero_for_missing cover passCount/pass_count/score.passedCriteria/score.passed and missing-default-0; identical helper used for fail and blocking variants.

evaluator.py:106-108 sets mcp_config = '.mcp.json' only when projectType=='web-frontend' AND .mcp.json exists; otherwise None; passed through to invoke_claude (the wired mcp_config kwarg accepted in invoke.py).
test_regression_returns_true_on_pass asserts invoke_regression() returns truthy on the pass scenario (mock writes regression/last-run.json with fail==0).

AST import scan over the 4 modules: every Import/ImportFrom resolves to either a stdlib module (json, os, shutil, pathlib, typing, ast, sys, __future__) or local 'harness' package. No third-party imports found.
git diff --name-only harness/sprint-03/pass..HEAD -- '*.sh' '*.ps1' returns no output; no shell scripts modified.
python tests/run-all.py layer1 exits 0 with 'Ran 117 tests in 27.678s' OK. Output references all seven module names: tests.layer1.test_{utils,invoke,git,planner,contract,generator,evaluator}.

Bats per-file counts (5,5,5,5)=20 are mirrored 1:1 by Python tests with names that map directly (returns_sprint_count, creates_product_spec_md, etc.). Python adds 8 tolerance-helper unit tests for parse_decision/count_criteria and 8 for _get_result/_get_count, totalling 5+17+5+13=40. No bats scenario was dropped. Mapping documented in generator-log.md.

test_utils (35), test_invoke (23), test_git (19) all included in the 117/117 OK run; counts match or exceed Sprint 3 baseline (Sprint 3 final counts were 35/22/19=76 -- one new test was added in test_invoke this sprint, no regressions).

regressionResults.evidence: All 77 prior-sprint layer1 tests (test_utils 35 + test_invoke 23 + test_git 19) remain green in the same run that exercises the new modules. No flakes, no skips.

scoreNotes: Correctness 9: every required tolerance variant is implemented and unit-tested; bash semantics for return codes (especially generator's 2-on-blocked) are preserved.
Robustness 9: helpers gracefully handle non-dict / missing fields by returning sentinel values ('unknown' / 0 / False); JSON parse errors are caught.
UX 8: log_phase/log_info/log_success/log_warn/log_error give clear narration of negotiation rounds and counts; CLI parity with bash is preserved.
DocsHelp 8: each module has a docstring listing its public API and tolerance contract; generator-log.md lists the bats->Python mapping.
PortabilityPackaging 9: stdlib-only imports verified via AST; uses pathlib and os.environ, no shelling out; cross-platform clean.
